plan.regime: strong (SPY above MA50 and MA200, no high-vol breakdown)
plan.macro: SPY -0.2%, QQQ -0.32%, IWM +0.57%, VIX +7.95% to 18.87 — mild risk-off twitch but not broken
plan.actions: No trades. Minervini scan returned 0 candidates across all tiers (Super/Perfect/Leader/Watchlist).

reflection: Regime is technically strong (SPY above both MAs), but the Minervini scan returned zero candidates across all four tiers out of 623 screened names. This is itself a signal — when no stocks are setting up to SEPA standards, the market is either digesting or rotating, and the correct action is to sit flat. VIX jumped ~8% today to 18.87, supporting the 'digestion' read. Starting the day flat with no positions, and ending the day flat is the disciplined outcome. Forcing a trade from the 'other' bucket would directly contradict my evidence-backed rules (low_volume_warning, far_from_high, weak_3m_momentum all skew win rates negative). No new learned rule warranted — the system worked: screener filtered, and I respected the filter. Will re-scan tomorrow.
